source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design the primary chassis frame of a two-person tandem human-powered planetary rover benchmark as a welded/bolted folding multi-tube assembly. Rover folds into a 5 ft x 5 ft x 5 ft (1.52 m x 1.52 m x 1.52 m) stowed cube at the start line, then unfolds to a deployed configuration for the course. Maximum empty rover mass (without crew) <= 113 kg (250 lb) per benchmark handbook. Two riders sit in tandem, each with harness-restrained seating and independent pedaling drivetrain to the ground wheels. Wheelbase in deployed configuration: 2.1 m, track width: 1.3 m, with four independently-sprung wheels each rated for an obstacle impact of 3 g over a 30 cm berm. Frame material: 6061-T6 aluminum tubing (Young's modulus 68.9 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3), primary tubes 38 mm OD x 1.6 mm wall. The assembly consists of the two longitudinal main rails (left + right), two lateral crossmembers tying the rails, two tandem seat sub-frames (front rider + rear rider), folding hinge joints at the fold axis, four wheel axle upright brackets, two pedal drivetrain mount brackets (front + rear riders), and a tool-task bracket where sampling arms attach. Course obstacles per benchmark handbook: 8 terrain features including crater edges, dune traverse at 10 deg slope, boulder field requiring ground clearance 20 cm, plus four tool-task stations where the rover stops and the crew operates on-board sampling arms. Load cases: (LC1) dual-crew loading: 2 x 85 kg = 170 kg crew + 113 kg rover = 283 kg total, 1 g static; (LC2) 30 cm berm crossing: 3 g vertical impulse on one wheel; (LC3) lateral slope: 10 deg side slope with full 283 kg weight, 0.17 g lateral; (LC4) pedaling reaction: 600 N horizontal at each pedal drive at 1.5 Hz for the 900-second course (1350 cycles per pedal); (LC5) tool-task reaction: 500 N vertical + 300 N horizontal at the tool-mount bracket on the frame during sampling; (LC6) stow-cube compliance: frame must fold into 5x5x5 ft cube without plastic deformation at hinge joints. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises in aluminum tubes under LC1-LC5 <= 184 MPa (yield 276 / 1.5); (R2) wheel-axle deflection under LC2 berm <= 10 mm; (R3) hinge-joint tube stress under fold/deploy cycle below 184 MPa; (R4) empty rover mass <= 113 kg (250 lb); (R5) first torsional frame mode above 10 Hz; (R6) stowed-cube envelope compliance verified geometrically. Verification is performed on the assembled welded/hinged tubular frame with representative weld and hinge-bushing stiffness; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP AP242, FEA per case, modal, mass report, and stow-cube compliance check.
short_prompt:
Design the primary chassis frame of a two-person tandem human-powered planetary rover benchmark as a welded/bolted folding multi-tube assembly.

Rover folds into a 5 ft x 5 ft x 5 ft (1.52 m x 1.52 m x 1.52 m) stowed cube. Empty rover mass <= 113 kg (250 lb). Deployed wheelbase 2.1 m, track 1.3 m; four wheels rated for 3 g over a 30 cm berm. Frame: 6061-T6 tubing (E 68.9 GPa, Poisson 0.33, yield 276 MPa, ultimate 310 MPa, density 2700 kg/m^3), primary tubes 38 mm OD x 1.6 mm wall. Ground clearance 20 cm; 10 deg dune traverse.

Load cases.
- LC1 dual-crew: 2 x 85 kg = 170 kg crew + 113 kg rover = 283 kg total at 1 g.
- LC2 berm: 3 g vertical impulse on one wheel crossing a 30 cm berm.
- LC3 lateral slope: 10 deg side slope with full 283 kg, 0.17 g lateral.
- LC4 pedaling: 600 N horizontal at each pedal drive at 1.5 Hz, 1350 cycles per pedal over the 900 s course.
- LC5 tool-task: 500 N vertical + 300 N horizontal at tool-mount bracket.
- LC6 stow-cube: frame folds into 5x5x5 ft without plastic deformation at hinges.

Wheel-axle deflection under LC2 berm <= 10 mm; first torsional mode > 10 Hz.

prompt.title: HERC two-crew folding human-powered rover frame (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 Al tandem rover frame: 2 main rails + 2 crossmembers + 2 seat sub-frames + folding hinges + 4 axle uprights + 2 pedal mounts + tool-task bracket, folding into 1.52 m cube.

parts.name: longitudinal_main_rail
parts.role: left / right longitudinal main rail of the tandem chassis

parts.name: lateral_crossmember
parts.role: lateral crossmember tying the two main rails at front and rear

parts.name: seat_subframe
parts.role: tandem seat sub-frame for front rider and rear rider

parts.name: folding_hinge_joint
parts.role: hinge joint on each main rail enabling fold into 5x5x5 ft stowed cube

parts.name: wheel_axle_upright
parts.role: upright bracket at each wheel station carrying the sprung axle

parts.name: pedal_drivetrain_mount
parts.role: bracket at each rider station carrying the pedal drivetrain reaction

parts.name: tool_task_bracket
parts.role: frame bracket carrying the sampling-arm tool-task reaction

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: deployed: 2.1 m wheelbase x 1.3 m track; stowed: 1.524 x 1.524 x 1.524 m cube
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X forward, Y transverse, Z up; fold axis transverse between seat stations

boundary_conditions.interfaces: main rails welded to crossmembers and seat sub-frames; hinge joints pinned along main rails with representative hinge bushing stiffness; wheel axle uprights welded + bolted to rails; pedal and tool brackets welded to rails
boundary_conditions.support: four wheel contact patches supported in LC1-LC5; hinge geometry verified kinematically in LC6

prompt.objective: minimize empty rover mass under 113 kg while passing yield, berm deflection, hinge, modal, and stow-cube gates

interfaces.load_path: lateral crossmembers welded into main rails
seat sub-frames welded into main rails
folding hinge joint on each main rail
wheel axle upright welded + bolted to main rail
pedal drivetrain mount welded to main rail
tool-task bracket welded to main rail

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: HERC rovers are tandem folding chassis with distinct rails, crossmembers, seat sub-frames, hinges, wheel uprights, and pedal / tool brackets; the fold-deploy and berm crossing load paths cross hinge and bracket interfaces that must be modelled explicitly.
notes.speculation_caveats: Hinge count, crossmember count, and bracket layout are speculative; materials, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
